source_jobs.title: Registration Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Location: Springfield Regional Outpatient Surgery Center, LLC

Address: 2610 N Limestone St, Springfield, OH 45503

Website: https://springfieldosc.com/

Springfield Regional Outpatient Surgery Center (Springfield OSC) is a Medicare-certified, AAAHC-accredited ambulatory surgery center located in Springfield, Ohio. With four operating rooms and a procedure room, the center provides high-quality, cost-effective outpatient surgical care across specialties including orthopedics, sports medicine, digestive health, general surgery, spine, women’s health, plastic surgery, podiatry, and pain management.

Owned and operated in partnership with local physicians, Springfield OSC combines advanced technology with compassionate, patient-centered care in a convenient setting.

Role Summary

The Registration Specialist supports a seamless perioperative experience by coordinating patient registration, and front desk operations. This role serves as a key liaison between patients, physicians, and clinical teams, ensuring accurate scheduling, registration, communication, and administrative support while maintaining a patient-centered, professional environment.

Monday-Friday, Full-time position (30-40 hours/week). Hours between 6:00am- 5:00pm.

What You’ll Do

 - Register patients accurately, verifying demographics, insurance, prior authorizations, and required documentation

 - Collect copayments and input patient information into scheduling and registration systems

 - Greet patients and visitors; answer phones and respond to in-person and telephone inquiries

 - Coordinate front desk and reception area activities to support efficient center operations

 - Assemble and maintain patient medical records and ensure confidentiality

 - Communicate professionally with patients, families, physicians, and staff (using translators as needed)

 - Support quality improvement, safety, and risk management initiatives

 - Maintain organization and cleanliness of the front desk and nourishment areas

 - Perform clerical and administrative duties, including supply management and record maintenance

 - Perform other duties as assigned

What You’ll Bring

 - High school diploma or GED required; healthcare patient experience preferred

 - Strong organizational, multitasking, and communication skills

 - Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced healthcare environment

 - Proficiency with Microsoft Windows–based systems and scheduling/registration platforms

Physical Requirements

 - Ability to lift up to 50 pounds

 - Prolonged standing, walking, sitting, and frequent use of hands and arms

 - Ability to bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, or balance as needed

Compass’ experienced leadership team has developed more than 250 ASCs over the past three decades, making it the partner of choice for high-performance ASCs. Orthopaedic Institute Surgery Center, located in Metropolis, Illinois, provides high-quality, patient-focused outpatient surgical care. With three ORs and dedicated minor procedure rooms, the center offers comprehensive orthopaedic services—from sports medicine and joint replacements to spine and plastic surgery. The result is a culture that values safety, efficiency, and compassion—for both patients and staff.

Role Summary:

The Perioperative Registered Nurse (Circulating Nurse) plays a critical role in managing patient care throughout the intraoperative experience.  - Assess patient needs preoperatively and intraoperatively

 - Develop and implement individualized care plans

 - Coordinate and communicate with the surgical team to maintain a safe, efficient OR environment

 - Monitor patient positioning, safety, and equipment use

 - Ensure accurate documentation and reporting

 - Support quality improvement and compliance initiatives

 - Uphold patient confidentiality and safety protocols

 - Graduate of an accredited nursing program

The Radiologic Technologist is responsible for performing high-quality intraoperative fluoroscopic imaging using C-arm technology in support of outpatient surgical procedures. Full-time, Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM–3:00 PM (30-40 hours/week).

• Perform fluoroscopy (C-arm) procedures during surgeries in accordance with physician requirements and safety standards

• Monitor and enforce radiation safety protocols for patients and staff.

• Participate in quality improvement and safety initiatives.
• Respond to emergency situations with competence and composure.

• Associate’s Degree in Applied Science (Radiologic Technology) or equivalent.
